generation_note: Hand-authored — generate-seed-evals.js emits sibling-skill-target prompts unsuitable for brain skills

target_selection.target: an unclear or general second-brain request needing diagnosis and routing

evals.prompt: Brain status — how's the project brain doing right now?

judgment: Runs brain-verify.js and brain-lint.js to diagnose structure and quality/security state
Reports a one-screen summary: structure OK, security findings, N candidates pending, days since last review
Routes to the correct next skill based on findings rather than performing a governed write itself

evals.prompt: Can you help me with our project memory setup? Not sure what state it's in or what needs doing.

judgment: Correctly identifies this as an unclear-operation request ('help me with project memory' is a listed router trigger) rather than assuming a specific operation
Diagnoses capsule state before recommending any specific next step
Routes based on findings table (e.g. no capsule -> project-brain-bootstrap, placeholder BRAIN.md -> second-brain-setup) rather than guessing

evals.prompt: Do something with the brain — I think we set one up a while back but I honestly don't remember.

workflow_steps.step: Run brain-verify.js and detect no capsule

judgment: Triggers on the vague 'do something with the brain' phrasing and runs diagnosis first rather than assuming state
When brain-verify.js finds no .project-brain/ capsule, routes to project-brain-bootstrap per the findings table instead of attempting any brain operation itself
Does not fabricate a status report when there is nothing to diagnose — clearly states no capsule exists

evals.prompt: What is the brain-kernel skill for, exactly? How is it different from the other brain skills?

judgment: Recognized as a meta-question about the skill itself, not a request to diagnose or route anything
Answers conversationally (router for unclear brain requests, diagnoses then routes, never performs governed writes) without invoking the skill

evals.prompt: It's been a while — can you give me a general sense of where things stand with our persistent project knowledge?

judgment: Synonym phrasing ('general sense', 'persistent project knowledge', 'it's been a while') activates the router without naming a specific operation
Surfaces days-since-last-review as part of the status report, which is directly relevant to the 'it's been a while' framing
Routes to brain-weekly-review if staleness is found (>7 days), per the findings table

evals.prompt: Compile the brain — take today's session notes and turn them into candidate entries for review.

judgment: The user names a specific operation ('compile the brain') — brain-kernel must NOT trigger here; this goes directly to brain-compile
Must not run a diagnosis-then-route cycle for a request that already unambiguously names its destination skill
A conversational handoff naming brain-compile directly (or letting brain-compile trigger) is correct; invoking brain-kernel's router workflow is the failure mode being tested

evals.prompt: How's the brain doing? We've been heads-down on skill-eval and agent-eval work in this Node.js toolkit and I haven't checked in on project memory in a while.

judgment: Triggers on the vague 'how's the brain doing' status check despite project-specific framing around skill-eval/agent-eval work
Runs scripts/brain/brain-verify.js and scripts/brain/brain-lint.js via Node.js, consistent with this project's script-only toolkit
Reports session-log activity relevant to the 'heads-down' period the user described, then routes appropriately (e.g. to brain-weekly-review if stale)

evals.prompt: We just finished the skill-eval cycle on brain-capture — now let's promote the candidates that came out of it.

judgment: The user names the specific operation 'promote the candidates' — this routes directly to /brain-promote, not through brain-kernel's diagnose-and-route cycle
brain-kernel correctly stays out of the way for an already-disambiguated request even when it follows naturally from a pipeline step (skill-eval) mentioned in project context
Suggesting the user run /brain-promote (since it is user-invoked only) without running brain-kernel's own diagnostic workflow is the correct behavior

evals.prompt: [Continuing from earlier in our session] We were talking about our second-brain setup for claude_code but got sidetracked. Let's pick back up — what's the overall state of things, and what should we do next?

judgment: Triggers correctly despite the resumption framing — 'what's the overall state of things, what should we do next' is still an unclear-operation request that needs diagnosis
Does not assume the earlier sidetracked topic implies a specific operation; still runs the diagnose step before recommending a route
Uses the established project_name (claude_code) in its status report rather than re-asking which project
